sourceRef.citation: Mustafa Adnan ali Ali et al. 2027

statement.en: Development of lipid-based nanoparticles formulation loaded with potential anticancer active compounds for future glioblastoma therapy
statement.summary: Cancer is responsible for approximately one out of every six deaths worldwide, making it a significant contributor to global mortality. There are approximately three million people living with cancer in the UK and the number predicted to increase by half a million by 2025. Glioblastoma (GB) is a grade IV brain tumours, it stands as the most prevalent and devastating form of brain tumours. Patients diagnosed with GB have a median survival of only 14.6 months following conventional treatment plan of surgery, radiation and chemotherapy. Passing through the blood-brain barrier (BBB) remain as a challenge when treating GBs, in addition to other limitation of the current treatment. Therefore, there is a need to develop a new carrier system, like nanoparticles (NPs) to improve the drug delivery.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a provisional research proposal (dated 2026, future-dated) for a formulation development study. The axiom describes a not-yet-executed experimental protocol. Glioblastoma drug delivery via lipid nanoparticles is an active, partially-validated research direction, but this specific formulation and its efficacy remain untested. Classification as FLOWING reflects provisional/preprint status and developmental stage.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 血液脳関門を突き破るナノ粒子が、時間とともに減衰する死亡率を逆転させる神秘的な比率。
